failure_modes.text: A decision can look safe locally while still creating dangerous downstream effects once it propagates through the broader system.
failure_modes.source_quote: Most useful when small interventions may have large downstream effects.
failure_modes.mitigation: Trace the plausible transmission path from the local decision through the broader system; if the propagation mechanism cannot be named, the concern is speculation.

failure_modes.text: First-order reasoning misses real risk when second- and third-order consequences matter more than the immediate result.
failure_modes.source_quote: Works when first-order reasoning is too shallow.
failure_modes.mitigation: Explicitly map second- and third-order consequences before accepting a first-order-only risk assessment.

failure_modes.text: Cascade thinking becomes noise when people invoke butterfly-effect language without naming a plausible transmission path.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger When Cascade Thinking Turns into Mysticism.
failure_modes.mitigation: Require a named mechanism for any claimed cascade; invoking butterfly-effect language without a plausible path is not analysis.

premortem_questions.text: If this small move goes wrong, through which specific chain of dependencies, delays, or feedback loops does the damage spread?
Which second- or third-order consequence would matter most if the obvious first-order outcome turns out to be the least important one?
premortem_questions.source_quote: The model compels us to engage in second-order thinking.

premortem_questions.text: What evidence would prove that our cascade story is grounded in a real transmission path rather than vague fear?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Danger When Cascade Thinking Turns into Mysticism.

heuristics.text: Trace consequence chains before declaring a local optimization safe.
heuristics.source_quote: An action at one point will invariably ramify to create an effect at a distant one.

heuristics.text: Prioritize the few cascade paths that matter most instead of trying to model every variable in the system.
heuristics.source_quote: The goal is to be a catalyst for action, not to be complete and comprehensive.

heuristics.text: Name the plausible transmission path or stop using butterfly-effect language.
heuristics.source_quote: Danger When Cascade Thinking Turns into Mysticism.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the chunks on cascade paths, higher-order effects, and anti-mysticism discipline.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader simulation and scenario-sprawl beyond the clearest consequence-tracing moves.
open_questions: The source supports scenario analysis and digital twins, but the Wave 2 surface stays on consequence propagation.

allies: The raw allies section is strongest on systems-thinking, root-cause-analysis, and scenario reasoning.
antagonists: The source is strongest against occams-razor, availability-heuristic, and substitution-bias framing.
structured_tensions: The clearest tensions are with status-quo-bias, representativeness-heuristic, and occams-razor.
